evals.prompt: I need to search the codebase for all places where we handle authentication tokens. I just want to understand the code, not change anything.
evals.expected_output: Recommends Explore agent type for read-only codebase search, using haiku model for speed and cost efficiency
expectations: Recommends Explore as the agent type (not Plan, not general-purpose)
Specifies subagent_type as 'Explore' in the Task call or recommendation
Does NOT recommend general-purpose since the task is read-only
May mention haiku model as appropriate for fast, cheap exploration
Does NOT suggest any write-capable agent for this read-only task

evals.prompt: I want to audit our Express.js API for SQL injection, XSS, and insecure dependencies. This is a security review, not a code change.
evals.expected_output: Recommends sdlc:security-reviewer plugin agent for vulnerability assessment
expectations: Recommends sdlc:security-reviewer as the agent type
Uses the plugin:agent naming convention (sdlc:security-reviewer, not just 'security-reviewer')
Does NOT recommend Explore or Plan since specialized security agent exists
Does NOT recommend general-purpose for a pure review task
Correctly identifies this as a review/audit task rather than implementation

evals.prompt: I have a 50,000-line Python file that needs to be analyzed for performance bottlenecks. The file is source code.
evals.expected_output: Recommends RLM pattern with swarm:rlm-code-analyzer for chunked source code analysis
expectations: Recommends swarm:rlm-code-analyzer (not rlm-chunk-analyzer or rlm-data-analyzer)
Mentions or implies chunked/RLM analysis for the large file
Does NOT recommend overriding the Haiku model for the analyzer
Mentions swarm:rlm-synthesizer for aggregating findings (Sonnet model)
Does NOT suggest a single Explore agent for a 50,000-line file analysis

evals.prompt: I need an agent to research current best practices for database connection pooling, then implement the changes in our codebase.
evals.expected_output: Recommends splitting into research phase (adr:adr-researcher or Explore) and implementation phase (general-purpose), since no single read-only agent can do both
expectations: Recognizes this requires both read-only research AND write capability
Does NOT recommend only Explore or Plan (they cannot write files)
Recommends general-purpose for the implementation phase or the combined task
May suggest adr:adr-researcher or feature-dev:code-explorer for the research phase
Clearly distinguishes that read-only agents cannot perform implementation work

evals.prompt: I have a directory with mixed files: some .py source files, a large CSV dataset, and several JSONL log files. I need to analyze all of them for anomalies.
evals.expected_output: Recommends multi-file RLM with different analyst types: rlm-code-analyzer for Python, rlm-data-analyzer for CSV, rlm-json-analyzer for JSONL
expectations: Recommends swarm:rlm-code-analyzer for the Python files
Recommends swarm:rlm-data-analyzer for the CSV files
Recommends swarm:rlm-json-analyzer for the JSONL files
Mentions multi-file RLM mode or simultaneous different analyst types
Recommends swarm:rlm-synthesizer (Sonnet) for aggregating all findings
Does NOT assign a single analyst type to all file types

evals.prompt: I need to run 'git log --oneline -20' and 'npm test' to check the project status. That's it.
evals.expected_output: Recommends Bash agent since the task only requires shell command execution
expectations: Recommends Bash as the agent type
Does NOT recommend general-purpose for simple command execution
Does NOT recommend Explore (which is for code reading, not command execution)
Recognizes that Bash agent has shell access only, which is sufficient here

evals.prompt: I need to refactor our authentication module. First analyze the architecture, then implement the changes, then run tests, then do a security check on the refactored code.
evals.expected_output: Recommends a pipeline of specialized agents: refactor:architect for analysis, refactor:refactor-code for implementation, refactor:refactor-test for testing, refactor:security-review for security validation
expectations: Recommends refactor:architect for the architecture analysis phase
Recommends refactor:refactor-code for the implementation phase
Recommends refactor:refactor-test for the test coverage phase
Recommends refactor:security-review for the security check phase
Uses correct plugin:agent naming convention for all agents
Does NOT use a single general-purpose agent for all four phases

evals.prompt: How do I configure MCP servers in Claude Code? I'm not sure about the syntax.
evals.expected_output: Recommends claude-code-guide agent which has read-only tools plus WebFetch and WebSearch for answering Claude Code questions
expectations: Recommends claude-code-guide as the agent type
Does NOT recommend Explore or Plan for a Claude Code usage question
Does NOT recommend general-purpose for a pure information question
Recognizes claude-code-guide has web access (WebFetch, WebSearch) for documentation lookup
